text: Per our discussion with in-house counsel, we should preserve all documents related to the litigation hold dated March 1, 2026.
This memo constitutes attorney work product prepared in anticipation of litigation.

This communication is privileged. The CEO and General Counsel should review before any external disclosure.

Subject to the litigation hold, please preserve all electronic records related to the Johnson matter.

text: I'm researching civil procedure rules for my law school class.

Please proofread this paragraph for grammar and clarity.

text: The case for adopting a remote-work policy is strong. We should privilege flexibility over rigid schedules.
notes: 'privilege' as a verb in HR context — false positive trap

text: The settlement of accounts at the end of the fiscal year requires careful reconciliation.
We need outside expertise. Counsel from a marketing consultancy would be valuable.
notes: 'outside' + 'counsel' separated by other words / different meaning

text: Confidential — for internal review only. The new product launch is on schedule.
notes: 'confidential' alone — should not trigger PRIVILEGE without legal markers
